basics.name: Basics
basics.description: Foundational information for getting started with kluster.ai, including installation across supported IDEs (Cursor, VS Code, Windsurf, Antigravity), review mode overview, workflow selection guidance, and core concepts like issue types and sensitivity levels.

ide_reviews.name: IDE Reviews
ide_reviews.description: Complete guide to running code reviews inside your IDE, covering both human-written and AI-generated code workflows. Includes on-demand reviews, automatic background reviews, dependency checks, and end-to-end examples for Cursor, VS Code, Windsurf, Antigravity, and Claude Code.

repo_reviews.name: Repo Reviews
repo_reviews.description: System-wide codebase analysis that detects cross-module bugs, silent error propagation, state inconsistencies, and assumption violations that single-file reviews miss. Covers setup via the web dashboard and kluster CLI, with GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket integration.

cli.name: CLI
cli.description: Using the kluster CLI for terminal-based code reviews, including installation, reviewing staged changes, diffs, and individual files. Also covers git hook integration for pre-commit and pre-push automation, repo reviews from the command line, and CI/CD pipeline usage.

configuration.name: Configuration
configuration.description: Customizing kluster.ai code review behavior, including sensitivity levels (Low through Critical), issue type scoping, analysis depth (Instant vs. Deep), custom rules, .klusterignore patterns for excluding files, and per-project settings.

external_knowledge.name: External Knowledge
external_knowledge.description: Connecting external project management tools like Jira to kluster.ai so code reviews can validate implementation against real ticket requirements and specifications. Covers setup, branch naming conventions, and prompt-based ticket detection for CLI tools.

troubleshooting.name: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.description: Solutions for common issues including installation errors, MCP timeouts, module import problems, and cache clearing. Also covers frequently asked questions about supported languages, IDE compatibility, team features, and the differences between Instant and Deep analysis.

reference.name: Reference
reference.description: Technical reference materials including the full MCP tools specification, response schema for programmatic integration, and CLI command reference with exit codes and output formatting options.
